title: Protocol Outcomes, Layers, Standards, and Evidence · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs protocol outcomes, layers, standards, and evidence from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.
Trace application policy, HTTP semantics, HTTP/1.1 syntax, TLS records, TCP byte streams, IP delivery, and local socket APIs without collapsing layers.
Use current RFC requirements, errata, Go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Outcome Evidence Contract · changed case · Review Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 6988 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.

Which action demonstrates Outcome Evidence Contract without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Outcome Evidence Contract · changed case · Review Outcome Evidence Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation. For Outcome Evidence Contract · changed case · Review Outcome Evidence Contract, keep Outcome Evidence Contract connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
